name: Sheri Portwood's Sour Cream Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and salt in the work bowl of a food processor and pulse several times to mix.
2. Cut the butter into 15 to 20 pieces and add it to the work bowl. Pulse repeatedly, about 20 times in all, to reduce the mixture to a fine powder. Check the consistency occasionally to avoid overprocessing.
3. Add the sour cream to the work bowl and pulse 5 or 6 more times, or until the dough forms a coherent ball. Do not overprocess.
4. Remove the dough from the work bowl and place it on a floured surface. Press it into two 6-inch disks.
5. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate. Keep the dough in the refrigerator for up to two days, or doublewrap in plastic and freeze for up to several weeks.
